g.text: Sudoku Solver — Minimal Test Case (2 Empty Cells)
Board is almost complete. Only [0][0] and [0][1] are empty ('.')
Initial Board:
Row 3: 2 1 4 | 3 6 5 | 8 9 7 (rows 3-8 fully filled, all valid)
isValid(board, row, col, c) — loop i=0..8, checks 3 things:
Check 1: board[i][col]==c → any cell in same COLUMN already has c?
Check 2: board[row][i]==c → any cell in same ROW already has c?
Check 3: board[3*(row/3)+i/3][3*(col/3)+i%3]==c → any cell in same 3x3 BOX has c?

svg.text: └── for i=0..8, j=0..8: scan every cell left-to-right, top-to-bottom
└── i=0,j=0: board[0][0]='.' → FOUND first empty cell!

g.text: STEP 1 — Empty cell found: [0][0] row=0, col=0

svg.text: └── for c='1' to '9': try each digit at [0][0]
│ board[5][0]=? board[6][0]=? board[7][0]=? board[8][0]=? → no '1' found ✓

g.text: │ All 3 checks pass → isValid returns TRUE ✓ → place c='1'

svg.text: │ i=0,j=1: board[0][1]='.' → FOUND second empty cell!

g.text: STEP 2 (inside recursion) — Empty cell: [0][1] row=0, col=1

svg.text: └── for c='1' to '9': try each digit at [0][1]

g.text: c='1': Check 2 (row 0): board[0][0]='1' == '1' → FAIL ✗ isValid returns false immediately
│ All pass → isValid TRUE ✓ → place c='2'

svg.text: │ i=1,j=0: '4' → skip ...all of row 1 filled → skip
│ i=2..8: all cells filled → skip skip skip...

g.text: → for loop completes (no '.' found) → return true ★
(base case: entire board filled = solved!)

svg.text: │ if(solve(board)) → YES → return true ← bubble up to STEP 1 caller

g.text: Back in STEP 1: inner solve() returned true
if(solve(board)) → YES → return true ← bubble up to solveSudoku()
solveSudoku() done — board is solved in-place
All other rows unchanged (already filled)
BONUS: What if c='1' at [0][0] led to a dead end? (Backtrack demo)
Modified board: [0][0]='.', [0][1]='2' (given) — so only '1' can go at [0][0]
Row 0: . 2 3 | 4 5 6 | 7 8 9 ← only [0][0] is empty, answer must be '1'
c='1': isValid(0,0,'1'): col ok, row ok (row has 2,3,4,5,6,7,8,9 no '1'), box ok → TRUE ✓ → place '1'

svg.text: board[0][0]='1' → recurse → no '.' found → return true → board solved! ✓
Now what if '1' was somehow invalid? (hypothetical)

g.text: c='2': isValid(0,0,'2'): Check 2 (row 0): board[0][1]='2' == '2' → FAIL ✗ skip
c='3': Check 2 (row 0): board[0][2]='3' == '3' → FAIL ✗ skip
c='4': Check 2 (row 0): board[0][3]='4' == '4' → FAIL ✗ skip
c='5','6','7','8','9': all in row 0 already → FAIL ✗ skip all
No c worked! → for(char c) loop ends without placing anything
return false ← BACKTRACK signal to parent (or solveSudoku returns with unsolved board)
Full call tree for original 2-empty-cell case
└── c='1': isValid=false (row has '1') ✗
└── c='2': isValid=true ✓ → board[0][1]='2'
└── return true // BASE CASE
if(solve()) → true → return true // CALL 2 returns true
if(solve()) → true → return true // CALL 1 returns true
Summary of every digit attempt
Cell [0][0]: c='1' → isValid=TRUE → placed → recurse into [0][1]
Cell [0][1]: c='1' → row has '1' → FAIL ✗
Cell [0][1]: c='2' → isValid=TRUE → placed → recurse → no '.' → return true
return true bubbles: CALL3 → CALL2 → CALL1 → solveSudoku()
Total calls: 3 recursive calls, 2 backtracks avoided (solved on first valid path)

svg.text: return true — board solved / bubbling up

g.text: Line-by-line code trace for each placement:
if(board[i][j]=='.') → TRUE at [0][0] → enter for(char c='1';c<='9';c++)
if(isValid(board,i,j,c)) → TRUE for c='1' → board[i][j]=c → if(solve(board)) → recurse
inner solve: all cells filled → for(i) loop ends → return true → if(solve()) true → return true
